channel.title: FuelWired — Oil & Gas Intelligence
channel.description: Real-time oil and gas industry news, energy market analysis, and LNG market intelligence.

item.title: Sable Seeks $1B Loan
item.description: The owner of the Santa Ynez Unit in California launched a marketing process for a senior secured term loan with a principal amount of up to $1 billion.

item.title: Equinor Targets 150,000 Boed Production Growth by 2030
item.description: The majority state-owned energy major plans 6-8 tieback projects offshore Norway each year.

item.title: ExxonMobil and ZET strike deal for South Africa’s first LNG terminal
item.description: The Zululand Energy Terminal (ZET) has executed a heads of agreement (HoA) with ExxonMobil South Africa LNG, an affiliate of ExxonMobil, to establish South Africa’s first liquefied natural gas (LNG) import terminal.

item.title: EnBW secures additional US LNG volumes from Venture Global
item.description: German energy company EnBW has secured additional US liquefied natural gas (LNG) volumes from Venture Global under new binding agreements for about 0.82 million tonnes per annum (mtpa).

item.title: Refinery Hit as Ukraine Pounds Moscow
item.description: Russia's capital faced a record air attack overnight, with drones reaching the Moscow Oil Refinery, disrupting airport operations and forcing closure of several major roads in and around the city.

item.title: Eskom Reports Sustained Improvements in Grid Reliability
item.description: The South African state-owned utility continues to register improvements in electricity supply reliability as winter demand escalates, reporting nearly 400 consecutive days without power interruptions from mid-May.

item.title: Australia’s Renewable Project Pipeline Is Booming
item.description: Following government tenders for nearly 10 gigawatts of new renewable energy capacity, Australia’s pipeline of probable clean energy projects jumped by about 30% to as much as 32.3 GW in the biggest surge on record. The…

item.title: US, Iran Peace Pact Takes Effect
item.description: President Donald Trump signed an interim deal to end the war with Iran and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, speeding up the timeline for the agreement to go into effect despite blowback from Republicans who said it amounted…

item.title: Argus: U.S-Iran Deal Won’t Lead to One-Way Traffic to Plunging Oil Prices
item.description: The uncertain pace of supply recovery in the Middle East and the continued rapid drawdowns of global inventories make the case that oil price volatility would drag on during the 60-day U.S.-Iran negotiation window…

item.title: Keyera takes full ownership of KAPS Pipeline in $860m deal
item.description: Keyera has acquired the remaining 50% non-operating interest in the KAPS Pipeline project in Canada from Stonepeak for C$1.21bn ($860m).

item.title: The future of bioleaching in mining
item.description: Advances in microbiology, growing demand for CRMs and the search for lower-emission processing are bringing bioleaching back into focus.

item.title: India’s Energy Import Bill Jumps 82% Due to High Oil Prices
item.description: India’s imports of crude and LNG jumped in May from April, as arrivals of non-Middle Eastern cargoes accelerated, and the country's energy import bill soared by nearly 82% from a year earlier amid higher energy prices…

item.title: More Than 60 Million Barrels of Oil Ready to Head to Asia as Hormuz Reopens
item.description: Crude cargo arrivals in Asia from the Middle East could accelerate in the coming weeks as more than 60 million barrels of oil stuck in the Persian Gulf prepare to exit the Strait of Hormuz and head to Asian markets once…

item.title: Goldman Sachs Warns Strait of Hormuz Traffic May Never Fully Recover
item.description: Tanker traffic via the Strait of Hormuz may never fully recover to pre-war levels thanks to alternative routes that oil producers in the Middle East have tapped amid the crisis, Goldman Sachs analysts have warned…

item.title: High Oil Prices Are Driving an EV Boom in Europe
item.description: High oil and fuel prices led to yet another monthly rise in electric car sales in Europe, at a solid 34% on the year, thanks in no small part to the wider availability of cheaper vehicles from China, Reuters reported…

item.title: Saudi Aramco Eyes $7 Billion Sulfur Asset Sale as Demand Soars
item.description: Saudi Arabia’s energy major Aramco is considering selling a stake in its sulfur business, eyeing proceeds of up to $7 billion, Reuters has reported, citing unnamed sources. The value of Aramco’s infrastructure assets…

item.title: Oil Prices Slide After U.S. and Iran Sign Ceasefire Agreement
item.description: Oil prices were under pressure in early Asian trading on Thursday after the U.S. and Iran formally signed an agreement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. The speed at which oil markets have changed their tune in recent…

item.title: Crude Stabilizes After Sharp Selloff
item.description: Oil edged higher, but prices remain under pressure as a US-Iran peace deal could restore major supplies to the market.

item.title: The Invisible Energy Crisis Threatening to Derail the AI Boom
item.description: Every AI boom forecast being published right now — every bull case, Big Tech earnings call, and valuation model — seems to be making the same assumption. The electricity will be there to power it when they need it. It…

item.title: State of Global Oil Inventories Ruins Iran Peace Optimism
item.description: As oil benchmarks crash and Brent slips below $80 per barrel, a growing number of analysts are sounding an alarm: Hormuz may reopen, but oil production in the region would not rebound immediately—and the world’s oil…
